description: Create the core Postgres schema and Rust data access layer for users, auth accounts, sessions, repositories, refs, commits, issues, pull requests, organizations, teams, Actions runs, packages, webhooks, and notifications.
ui_details: No direct UI. This backs every Next.js page and must expose stable JSON shapes for app shell, repository, issue, PR, profile, organization, and settings screens.
behavior: Migrations run through SQLx against Postgres. Foreign keys enforce ownership and permissions. Seed no mock data; create records only through real auth/API flows.
data_model: Writes SQLx migrations under crates/api/migrations for users, auth_accounts, sessions, repositories, repository_git_refs, commits, issues, pull_requests, labels, organizations, teams, actions_workflows, workflow_runs, packages, webhooks, notifications.

description: Implement Google OAuth login with Better Auth and an opengithub-styled compact sign-in page.
ui_details: Centered narrow auth card on neutral background. Octocat-style mark at top, heading 'Sign in to opengithub', single full-width primary button 'Continue with Google', small terms/privacy links below. No email/password fields.
behavior: Unauthenticated protected routes redirect to /login?next=<url>. Clicking Continue with Google starts Better Auth OAuth. Successful callback creates/updates user profile and redirects to next URL or /dashboard. Failed callback shows inline error in the card.
data_model: Reads/writes Better Auth users, auth_accounts, and sessions tables. Stores Google provider account id, email, display name, avatar URL, session expiry, created_at, updated_at.

description: Protect Next.js routes and Rust API routes with shared session verification.
ui_details: Protected UI pages show logged-in avatar/menu in the header. Unauthenticated users see sign-in CTA. API errors render through existing page-level error components.
behavior: Next.js middleware checks Better Auth session for protected pages. Rust Axum middleware validates Better Auth session cookie or bearer token. Protected API calls return 401 JSON when missing, 403 when authenticated but unauthorized.
data_model: Reads sessions, users, repository permissions, organization memberships, team memberships, and personal access tokens when token auth is added.

description: Create first-run dashboard onboarding for users with no repositories.
ui_details: Signed-in dashboard empty state inside the global app shell. Left column has a 'Top repositories' panel with an empty list, repository search input, and green 'New' button. Main column has a bordered welcome/get-started panel with CTAs for Create repository, Import repository, and Read setup guide. Use Primer-style 6px radius cards, #0969da blue links/buttons, #1f883d green create button, #d0d7de borders, and compact 14px body text.
behavior: After first Google login, users with zero repositories land on this empty state. Create repository opens /new. Import repository opens /new/import placeholder. Read setup guide opens local docs/get-started page. Dismiss buttons hide individual hints and persist per user. Once a repository exists, dashboard switches from empty welcome to recent activity and repository feed.
data_model: Reads current user, repositories owned by user/org memberships, recent activity, assigned issues, review requests, and dismissed_onboarding_hints. Writes dashboard_hint_dismissals with user_id, hint_key, dismissed_at.

description: Implement the signed-in personal dashboard layout and top-repository sidebar.
ui_details: Two-column dashboard under the compact signed-in header. Left sidebar is 296px desktop width with 'Top repositories' heading, green 'New' button, repository filter input, and a vertical repo list with 16px owner/repo text, visibility badge, language color dot, and updated time. Main feed column is fluid, max 720px, with stacked activity cards. Right rail is optional for later recommendations on wide screens.
behavior: GitHub mark routes here after login. Repository filter narrows the sidebar list client-side after server data loads. Clicking a repository opens /{owner}/{repo}. New opens /new. Empty sidebar shows first-run CTAs. Mobile stacks sidebar above feed and keeps controls reachable without horizontal scroll.
data_model: Reads current user, repositories ranked by direct interaction, organization memberships, team memberships, recent_visits, repository languages, repository visibility, and latest repository activity timestamp.

description: Show dashboard recent activity for issues and pull requests involving the user.
ui_details: Recent activity section at the top of the main dashboard feed. Show up to four compact rows/cards with issue or pull request icon, repository full name, title, number, state badge, updated relative time, and small actor avatar. Use muted gray metadata and blue linked titles.
behavior: Preview the four most recent qualifying issue/PR updates from the last two weeks. Clicking the title navigates to the issue or pull request. Empty state says there is no recent activity and offers Create repository and Explore repositories links. Activity updates after issue/PR comments, assignments, review requests, state changes, and mentions.
data_model: Reads issues, pull_requests, comments, review_requests, assignments, mentions, repository permissions, users, and activity_events filtered to current user and updated_at >= now - 14 days.

description: Build the dashboard feed with Following/For you filters and event-type filtering.
ui_details: Main dashboard feed below recent activity. Header has segmented tabs or filter pills for Following and For you, plus a right-aligned Filter dropdown with checkbox event types. Feed cards include actor avatar, action summary, repository link, excerpt/title, timestamp, and optional star/follow action.
behavior: Following shows activity from followed users and watched repositories. For you shows recommendations from starred repositories, contributed repositories, collaborators, and followed organizations. Filter dropdown limits event types such as stars, follows, repository creation, help-wanted issues/PRs, pushes, forks, and releases. Empty filtered state offers Clear filters.
data_model: Reads follows, repository_watches, stars, forks, releases, issues, pull_requests, commits, organizations, users, repositories, and feed_events. Writes no records except optional feed preference/filter persistence.

description: Create repository flow modeled on GitHub's live /new form.
ui_details: Centered Primer-style form under signed-in header. Page title 'Create a new repository', helper text, inline 'Import a repository' link. General section has owner dropdown, required repository name input with suggestion button, optional description input with 350-character counter. Configuration section has visibility dropdown with Public/Private descriptions, template dropdown, README On/Off toggle, searchable .gitignore selector dialog, license dropdown, and green primary 'Create repository' button.
behavior: Owner dropdown lists personal account and writable organizations. Repository name input shows suggested names and previews normalized names when spaces are typed, while server validation remains authoritative. Visibility menu toggles Public/Private. README toggle changes pressed state immediately. .gitignore selector opens searchable listbox of templates. Submit creates repository through Rust API; if README/template/gitignore/license are selected, bootstrap default branch and first commit. Success redirects to /{owner}/{repo}; validation and availability errors remain inline without clearing the form.
data_model: Reads users, organizations, memberships, repository_creation_templates, gitignore_templates, license_templates, name availability, and owner permissions. Writes repositories, repository_git_refs, commits, git trees/blobs or object storage records, repository feature flags, default labels, and repository_activity_events.

description: Import an external Git repository into a new opengithub repository.
ui_details: Wizard-style form titled 'Import your project to opengithub'. Step content uses compact labels and explanatory text. Fields include source repository URL, optional private-source credentials, destination owner dropdown, destination repository name input, Public/Private visibility dropdown, and green 'Begin import' button. After submission, show a status page with repository name, progress message, spinner/progress state, and completion/error callout.
behavior: User enters a public Git remote URL, optionally provides credentials for private remotes, chooses owner/name/visibility, then clicks Begin import. API creates a repository and queued import job, then redirects to a preparing/status page. Status polls until queued/importing/imported/failed. Completion links to /{owner}/{repo} and sends an email. Errors explain unreachable sources, invalid credentials, unsupported non-Git repositories, duplicate names, and private-network sources. Do not import issues, pull requests, metadata, or LFS objects in MVP.
data_model: Reads users, organizations, memberships, owner permissions, repository name availability, and repository_imports. Writes repositories, repository_imports, repository_import_credentials secret references, repository_git_refs, commits, git object storage records, repository_activity_events, and notification/email job records.

description: Render the repository Code tab overview with header actions, repository tabs, file table, README/sidebar metadata, and latest commit summary.
ui_details: Full-width repository workspace under signed-in app shell. Header shows owner/repo breadcrumb, repository name, Public/Private badge, Watch/Fork/Star buttons with counts, and horizontal tabs for Code, Issues, Pull requests, Discussions, Actions, Security, Insights, and permission-gated Settings. Code toolbar has branch selector, branch/tag counts, Go to file combobox, Add file dropdown, and Code clone/download button. File table rows use folder/file icons, name/path, latest commit message with PR links, and relative date. Right sidebar shows About text, website link, topics, resources, stars/watchers/forks, releases, deployments, contributors, and language bar.
behavior: Loading /{owner}/{repo} resolves default branch and renders root tree. Clicking tabs navigates within repo context. Clicking folder/file opens tree/blob route. Clicking latest commit opens commit detail. History opens commit history for current path. Watch/Fork/Star update counts optimistically then reconcile from API. Empty repositories render quick setup instead of file table.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_git_refs, commits, git_trees, repository_topics, repository_languages, stars, repository_watches, forks, releases, deployments, contributors, and repository_readmes. Writes stars/watches/forks and repository_activity_events.

description: Implement repository branch/tag selection, Go to file, and directory tree browsing.
ui_details: Repository tree view uses split-pane layout with optional collapsible left file tree and draggable splitter. Header shows branch button, breadcrumbs, current directory title, Go to file combobox, Add file dropdown, More options, latest commit summary, History link, and table columns Name, Last commit message, Last commit date. Nested directories include a parent directory row and compact breadcrumb path.
behavior: Branch selector opens searchable branch/tag menu and reloads the same path at the selected ref. Go to file searches repository paths and opens the selected file. Directory rows navigate to tree routes; file rows navigate to blob routes. Invalid refs or missing paths show repository-scoped 404 with a branch/path recovery link. Large directories paginate or virtualize without layout shift.
data_model: Reads repository_git_refs, git_trees, commits, code_search_index path records, repository_permissions, and users for commit authors. Writes no records except optional recent_visits for opened repositories/paths.

description: Render repository blob/file views with syntax highlighting, line numbers, raw/copy/download actions, history, blame, and symbol entry points.
ui_details: Blob view keeps repository split-pane shell. Header shows branch selector, breadcrumbs, file name, Go to file, latest commit actor/message/SHA/time, History link, metadata text like '352 lines (352 loc) · 15.9 KB', Code and Blame toggles, Raw link, copy raw button, download button, symbol pane button, and More file actions. Code viewer uses monospace text, line numbers, syntax highlighting, line-hover anchors, and a read-only hidden textarea for accessibility.
behavior: Opening a file resolves the selected ref and renders text files with syntax highlighting. Raw streams raw bytes. Copy copies raw content or selected permalink. Download saves the raw file. Blame toggles to line-by-line commit attribution. Large/binary files show a non-renderable state with Raw/Download. Keyboard shortcuts include y for permalink, b for blame, and l for line jump.
data_model: Reads git_objects, git_trees, commits, repository_git_refs, code_search_index, repository_permissions, users, and optional symbol index records. Writes recent_visits and optional code navigation cache records.

description: Support HTTPS Git clone/fetch/push, raw file streaming, and source archive downloads for repositories.
ui_details: Code menu popover on repository pages with HTTPS clone URL input, copy button, Download ZIP action, and disabled/hidden SSH tab until SSH keys ship. Empty repositories show Quick setup commands for clone, creating README, and pushing an existing repository. Developer docs show git remote examples using opengithub.namuh.co URLs.
behavior: Copy clone URL writes HTTPS URL to clipboard. Git clients can clone public repositories anonymously and private repositories with session/token auth. Authorized users can fetch and push over smart HTTP. Download ZIP generates or reuses an archive for the selected ref. Raw/archive endpoints enforce visibility and stream bounded responses.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_git_refs, git_objects, git_trees, commits, personal_access_tokens, and repository_archives. Writes git_objects, git_trees, commits, repository_git_refs, repository_archives, repository_activity_events, and audit log records on push.

description: Expose initial GitHub-style REST API for authenticated user, repository create/detail, repository issues, pull requests, Actions runs, packages, and code search.
ui_details: API docs page later shows endpoint cards with method badges, path, request JSON, response JSON, auth requirements, and pagination examples. No standalone SDK package in initial scope.
behavior: REST routes return opengithub-owned data with consistent JSON envelopes. List endpoints accept page/pageSize and return items/total/page/pageSize. Errors use code/message envelope and correct HTTP status.
data_model: Reads/writes users, repositories, issues, pull_requests, workflow_runs, packages, code search indexes. Auth middleware reads sessions and future personal access tokens.

description: Document developer workflows equivalent to GitHub CLI quickstart without building a separate SDK package.
ui_details: Developer settings page with Personal access tokens card, webhook/API documentation links, and command snippets in monospace blocks. Docs page uses endpoint tables and copy buttons.
behavior: Users can read clone/push/API examples and later create tokens. Copy buttons copy commands. Token-protected examples use opengithub domain and never GitHub API URLs.
data_model: Reads users and repositories. Later writes personal_access_tokens with token hash, name, scopes, last_used_at, expires_at.

description: Implement the signed-in app shell with GitHub-style global navigation, quick links, avatar menu, and responsive page frame.
ui_details: Compact sticky header with GitHub mark, hamburger/global menu button, search/jump input, Create plus button, Issues/Pull requests/Notifications icon links, and 32px avatar menu. Content area uses centered max-width containers for dashboards and full-width repository workspaces. Mobile collapses nav into the hamburger drawer.
behavior: GitHub mark navigates to /dashboard. Hamburger opens a drawer with recent repositories, teams, projects, and primary app links. Avatar menu opens profile/settings/sign-out actions. Protected pages redirect unauthenticated users to /login?next=<url>.
data_model: Reads current user, avatar URL, unread notification count, recent repositories, organization memberships, team memberships, and recent projects. Writes only sign-out/session revocation through auth flow.

description: Create the global site map routes and navigation skeleton for dashboard, repository workspaces, profiles, organizations, search, notifications, and settings.
ui_details: Navigation skeleton follows sitemap.md. Repository pages share an owner/repo breadcrumb header, visibility badge, action buttons, and horizontal tabs. Settings pages share a left sidebar with active item highlighting and right-column form/card content.
behavior: Routes render real pages or explicit not-yet-built empty states, never 404s for known sitemap entries. Repository tab clicks preserve owner/repo context. Settings sidebar links update active section and maintain form state only inside each section.
data_model: Reads route params owner, repo, branch, issue number, pull number, org, team_slug, search query, and settings section. Reads repository permissions to decide whether Settings appears.

description: Implement GitHub-style global search and jump-to navigation across repositories, code, issues, pull requests, commits, users, and organizations.
ui_details: Header search input styled as compact rounded command field. Focus opens a suggestion popover with recently visited repositories, teams, projects, and query suggestions. Results page has search input, type tabs, filter sidebar, result count, highlighted snippets, and pagination.
behavior: Typing owner/repo suggestions allows direct navigation. Pressing Enter submits to /search?q=<query>&type=repositories by default. Type tabs switch between repositories, code, issues, pull requests, commits, users, and discussions. Empty results show a helpful no-results state with syntax tips.
data_model: Reads repositories, users, organizations, issues, pull_requests, commits, code_search_index, and recent_visits. Uses Postgres pg_trgm and full-text indexes; code results include repository, path, line, fragment, and matching ranges.

description: Build the logged-out public home page and global marketing navigation as a low-priority acquisition surface.
ui_details: Public header with GitHub mark, dropdown mega-nav groups, search/jump field, Sign in link, bordered Sign up button, large hero, email/signup CTA, feature sections, customer story cards, and dense multi-column footer. Use Primer-like neutral colors, blue/green accents, dark text, and responsive stacked mobile layout.
behavior: Dropdown nav opens on click/hover and closes on outside click or Escape. Search field routes to /search. Sign in routes to /login and Sign up routes to /login for opengithub Google-only auth. Footer links route to implemented pages or static placeholders.
data_model: Reads no private data. Optional static content table for marketing sections, feature cards, customer stories, and footer link groups. Search submit reads query only.

description: Render repository issue list with query search, open/closed tabs, saved URL filters, metadata badges, and pagination/infinite result loading.
ui_details: Repository Issues tab uses the shared repository header/tabs, then a dismissible contributor guidance banner, page heading, Primer-style search builder input, Labels and Milestones links, green New issue button, Open/Closed count tabs, filter toolbar, and dense issue rows. Each row shows open/closed icon, title with inline code formatting support, colored label pills with tooltip descriptions, issue number, repo context, author, relative opened/updated time, linked PR indicator, and comment count.
behavior: Default query is `is:issue state:open`. Typing or choosing filters updates the query and URL. Open/Closed tabs switch state filters. Row title opens the issue detail. Dismiss hides the contributor banner for that viewer/repository. Empty results show a no-results state with clear-query affordance. Public repositories allow read access; private repositories require permission.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, issues, issue_comments_count/materialized counts, users, labels, milestones, projects, issue_types, pull_requests linked by closing/reference relationships, and repository_issue_preferences. Writes recent_visits and optional dismissed issue-list banner preference.

description: Implement issue filter menus and sort controls for author, labels, projects, milestones, assignees, issue type, comments, creation date, update date, best match, and reactions.
ui_details: Filter toolbar uses compact dropdown buttons: Author, Labels, Projects, Milestones, Assignees, Types, and Sort by. Label menu is a popover/dialog with a search combobox, multiselect listbox, color swatches, label names, descriptions, and a No labels option. Sort menu is a radio menu grouped into Sort by and Order, with checked state and keyboard shortcuts shown in accessible metadata.
behavior: Opening a filter menu focuses its combobox. Typing narrows options. Selecting a value appends or replaces the corresponding search qualifier such as `label:Bug`; Alt-click on labels excludes labels. Sort choices update `sort:` in the query and refresh results without losing other filters. Invalid advanced queries show inline warnings and preserve typed text.
data_model: Reads labels, milestones, users with repository access, project links, issue_types, issue aggregate counts, reactions, and issue search index records. Writes no issue data; only updates URL/query state and optional recent filter telemetry.

description: Create repository issues through template chooser, issue forms, Markdown editors, validation, attachment affordances, and query-parameter prefill.
ui_details: New issue opens a modal/route headed `Create new issue` with template cards when templates exist. Template form uses a required title input, explanatory template body, text inputs, Markdown textarea sections with Write/Preview tabs and formatting toolbar, attachment drop zones, required field markers, Create more checkbox, Cancel button, and green Create button with Command+Enter hint.
behavior: Selecting a template loads the configured issue form fields. Required title and required template fields block Create until valid. Preview renders Markdown or `Nothing to preview`. Cancel returns to the list without creating. Create persists the issue, applies template-provided labels/type/assignees when authorized, optionally keeps the form open when Create more is checked, and redirects to the new issue detail.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, issue_templates, issue_form_fields, labels, milestones, users, projects, and current user. Writes issues, issue_body_versions, issue_comments initial body, issue_labels, issue_assignees, issue_project_items, issue_timeline_events, attachments metadata, and notifications.

description: Render issue detail conversations with timeline events, comments, Markdown, reactions, linked PRs, metadata sidebar, subscriptions, and close/reopen/comment actions.
ui_details: Issue detail keeps repository tabs and shows title, issue number, status pill, navigation buttons, New issue action, main timeline column, and right metadata sidebar. Timeline cards show avatar, author, role badge, timestamp, action kebab, Markdown body with code blocks/copy buttons, reaction toolbar, label events, comments, linked PR/commit events, and composer. Sidebar sections include Assignees, Labels, Type, Fields, Projects, Milestone, Relationships, Development, Notifications, Participants, and Issue actions.
behavior: Comment composer supports Write/Preview, Markdown toolbar, attachment drop zone, saved replies shortcut, Comment submit, and Close issue. Preview renders Markdown. Reactions toggle per emoji. Subscribe/Customize changes notification state. Authorized users can close/reopen, edit metadata, assign labels/assignees/milestone/projects, pin/transfer/delete where permission allows. Linked PRs and closing keywords create timeline references.
data_model: Reads issues, issue_comments, issue_timeline_events, users, labels, milestones, issue_types, issue_fields, projects, issue_relationships, pull_requests, commits, reactions, subscriptions, participants, repository_permissions, and attachments. Writes issue_comments, reactions, subscriptions, issue state changes, timeline events, metadata link tables, notification deliveries, and audit records.

description: Render repository pull request list with query search, open/closed tabs, review/check metadata, draft state, labels, linked issues, and pagination.
ui_details: Repository Pull requests tab uses the shared repository header/tabs, dismissible contributor guidance banner, Primer-style search builder defaulting to `is:pr is:open`, Labels and Milestones links, green New pull request button, Open/Closed count tabs, filter toolbar, and dense rows. Rows show PR state or Draft icon, title, label pills, number, opened time, author, role badge, check-run summary icon, review status pill, linked issue count, task progress, and comments count.
behavior: Default query lists open PRs. Open/Closed tabs update state filters. Clicking a row opens /pull/{number}. New pull request opens compare/create. Dismiss hides contributor banner for the viewer. Check/review/comment/linked-issue indicators navigate to the relevant PR detail sections. Pagination keeps query and sort state in the URL.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, pull_requests, users, labels, pull_request_reviews, pull_request_checks_summary, pull_request_review_requests, linked issues, task list progress, comments counts, and repository pull-list preferences. Writes recent_visits and optional dismissed PR-list banner preference.

description: Implement pull request filter menus and sort controls for author, label, projects, milestones, reviews, assignee, comments, updates, best match, and reactions.
ui_details: Filter toolbar uses compact dropdown buttons: Filters, Author, Label, Projects, Milestones, Reviews, Assignee, and Sort. Reviews menu is a radio popover with No reviews, Review required, Approved review, Changes requested, Reviewed by you, Not reviewed by you, Awaiting review from you, and Awaiting review from you or your team. Sort menu contains Newest, Oldest, Most/Least commented, Recently/Least recently updated, Best match, and emoji reaction sorts.
behavior: Opening any menu focuses its filter/search field when present. Selecting options mutates the search-builder query and URL without losing other qualifiers. Review filters map to review state qualifiers and review request state. Sort choices refresh the list in place. Invalid advanced queries show inline validation and preserve typed text.
data_model: Reads pull_requests, users, labels, milestones, project links, review states, review requests, comments counts, reactions, checks summaries, and PR search index records. Writes no PR data; updates URL/query state and optional recent filter telemetry.

description: Create pull requests through compare/base-head branch selection, fork comparison, template-prefilled form, draft mode, labels, milestones, assignees, and reviewers.
ui_details: Compare page headed `Comparing changes` with compare-across-forks button, base branch/tag selector, compare branch/tag selector, swap/diff direction affordance, split/unified diff controls, changed-file summary, empty/no-diff callout, sample comparison links, and create form when refs differ. Create form uses title input, Markdown body editor with template content, draft checkbox, reviewers/assignees/labels/milestone selectors, and green Create pull request button.
behavior: Selecting different refs recomputes commits and files. Same base/head shows `There isn't anything to compare`. Invalid or missing refs show repository-scoped errors. Creating persists the PR, links closing keywords to issues, applies metadata when authorized, optionally marks draft, requests reviews, and redirects to /pull/{number}. Public fork PRs require permission checks and preserve base repository notifications.
data_model: Reads repository_git_refs, commits, git merge base, pull_request_templates, users, teams, labels, milestones, repository_permissions, forks, and existing open PRs for duplicate detection. Writes pull_requests, pull_request_commits, pull_request_files, pull_request_review_requests, pull_request_timeline_events, linked issue relationships, notifications, and audit records.

description: Render pull request conversation with timeline events, review requests, checks summary, mergeability box, sidebar metadata, comments, and notifications.
ui_details: PR detail keeps repository tabs and shows title, number, Open/Draft/Merged/Closed state pill, stack navigation when present, author, base/head refs, line-change counts, Preview/View status/Code controls, tabs for Conversation/Commits/Checks/Files changed, main timeline column, merge box, comment composer, and right metadata sidebar. Timeline cards show body/comments, bot comments with collapsible details, label events, force-push/base-change/ready-for-review/review-request/review events, commits, reactions, and review approval/change-request status.
behavior: Conversation tab loads PR state and timeline. Comment composer supports Write/Preview, Markdown toolbar, attachments, saved replies, and Comment. Subscribe/Customize updates notifications. Authorized users can edit metadata, request reviewers, mark draft/ready, close/reopen, and merge when checks/reviews/rules allow. Merge box blocks draft PRs, conflicts, unmet required checks, missing reviews, branch protection, rulesets, and stacked PR failures with explicit reasons.
data_model: Reads pull_requests, pull_request_commits, pull_request_reviews, pull_request_review_requests, pull_request_checks_summary, pull_request_timeline_events, issue_comments, users, labels, milestones, projects, branch_protection_rules, rulesets, subscriptions, participants, reactions, repository_permissions, and linked issues. Writes comments, reactions, subscriptions, metadata changes, review requests, state changes, merge records, notifications, and audit records.

description: Implement pull request Files changed diff review with file tree, split/unified hunks, viewed tracking, inline comments, and submit review dialog.
ui_details: Files changed tab has sticky PR toolbar, viewed progress, Submit review button, diff settings, commit selector, file filter input, resizable file tree, per-file header, additions/deletions summary, Viewed toggle, file actions, and diff table. Diff rows include old/new line columns, hunk headers, expand-up/down buttons, syntax-highlighted added/removed/context lines, and inline comment affordances. Submit review dialog has Markdown summary editor, Write/Preview tabs, attachment affordance, radio choices Comment/Approve/Request changes, Cancel, and Submit review with Command+Enter hint.
behavior: File filter narrows changed files. File tree jumps to a file. Viewed toggles collapse and persists per user/file version, resetting when the file changes. Split/unified and whitespace options rerender the diff. Line comments create pending review comments; pending comments remain private until Submit review. Submit review publishes comments and emits comment/approval/request-changes state. Abandoning/canceling leaves no published comments.
data_model: Reads pull_request_files, diff hunks from git object storage, pull_request_viewed_files, pull_request_review_comments, pending review drafts, users, repository_permissions, and syntax metadata. Writes pull_request_viewed_files, pending review comments, pull_request_reviews, published review comments, timeline events, notifications, and audit records.

description: Support pull request merge methods, mergeability computation, checks integration, plaintext diff/patch views, and Git refs needed by web and API clients.
ui_details: Merge box displays required review/check/ruleset status, conflicts, branch protection messages, enabled merge method dropdown, commit title/body confirmation fields, and branch delete option when applicable. Developer affordances include `.diff` and `.patch` URL hints plus API docs for list/create/review/merge endpoints.
behavior: API computes merge base, diff, conflicts, required review state, status-check state, and enabled merge methods. Authorized users can merge with merge commit, squash, or rebase when repository settings permit. Merge writes Git objects/refs atomically, closes linked issues via keywords, updates PR state, optionally deletes the head branch, and returns 409 with structured blockers when merge is not allowed.
data_model: Reads pull_requests, repository_git_refs, commits, git_objects, pull_request_reviews, workflow_runs/check_runs, branch_protection_rules, rulesets, repository merge settings, issue relationships, and repository_permissions. Writes commits, repository_git_refs, pull_request merge state, issue closures, notifications, repository_activity_events, and audit records.

description: Render repository Actions all-workflows run list with workflow rail, filters, run rows, statuses, and management navigation.
ui_details: Repository workspace tab content with a left Actions rail and main run list. Left rail heading 'Actions' lists All workflows, pinned workflows with pin icons, additional workflow names/files, Show more workflows button, then Management links for Caches, Deployments, Attestations, Usage metrics, and Performance metrics. Main pane has 'All workflows' heading, filter search input, run count, compact filter buttons for Workflow, Event, Status, Branch, Actor, and dense run rows with status icon, title, workflow, run number, event/commit/PR, actor avatar, branch pill, relative time, duration/live state, and kebab options.
behavior: Loading /actions fetches workflows and recent runs. Typing in Filter workflow runs updates the list and URL query. Filter buttons open searchable select-panel dialogs; status supports action required, cancelled, completed, failure, in progress, neutral, queued, skipped, stale, success, timed out, waiting. Clicking a workflow scopes the run list. Clicking a run opens /actions/runs/{run_id}. Empty repo with no workflows shows template cards and New workflow CTA.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, actions_workflows, workflow_runs, workflow_jobs summary counts, check_suites/check_runs, users, repository_git_refs. Writes only recent view/filter telemetry in MVP.

description: Render workflow-specific run history and manual workflow dispatch controls.
ui_details: Same Actions rail with selected workflow highlighted. Main pane heading is workflow name, followed by source workflow file link, workflow options menu, optional Run workflow button, filter search, Event/Status/Branch/Actor filter buttons, and scoped run rows. Run workflow dialog has branch dropdown and dynamically generated inputs using compact label/input/select/checkbox controls.
behavior: Selecting a workflow scopes run history to that workflow and removes the Workflow filter. The source file link opens the workflow YAML blob. Run workflow appears only when the default-branch workflow defines workflow_dispatch; submitting validates ref and inputs, queues a run, closes the dialog, and inserts the queued run at the top. Invalid YAML shows an invalid workflow callout and disables dispatch.
data_model: Reads actions_workflows, workflow_dispatch_inputs parsed from YAML, workflow_runs, repository_git_refs, users, repository_permissions. Writes workflow_runs, workflow_run_attempts, workflow_jobs seed records, check_suites/check_runs, queue messages for dispatch runs.

description: Render workflow run detail with summary, job sidebar, annotations, artifacts, rerun, cancel, and delete-log actions.
ui_details: Run detail header shows workflow name breadcrumb, large status icon/conclusion, run title, run number, run attempt selector where applicable, triggering commit/PR/branch metadata, and action menus for rerun/cancel/delete logs. Body is split: scrollable left sidebar with Summary, All jobs, grouped/collapsible job categories, and many job links; main summary cards show annotations, job summaries, artifact table with name/digest/copy/download, and run metadata.
behavior: Opening a run fetches run, jobs, annotations, artifacts, and attempts. Sidebar job links route/focus to a job log. Rerun all/failed/specific jobs queue new attempts when authorized and under limit. Cancel appears for queued/in-progress runs. Delete logs removes log storage and hides log-download options after confirmation. Artifact download returns short-lived signed URL.
data_model: Reads workflow_runs, workflow_run_attempts, workflow_jobs, workflow_steps summaries, workflow_annotations, workflow_artifacts, check_suites/check_runs, users, commits, pull_requests, repository_permissions. Writes rerun/cancel/delete-log audit records, new run attempts/jobs, log deletion markers, queue messages.

description: Implement job log viewer with collapsible steps, line search, log options, and downloadable archives.
ui_details: Job view keeps the run left sidebar. Main panel shows job title/status, duration, annotations toggle, log search input with previous/next result icon buttons, result count, log options dropdown, and a vertical list of collapsible step rows. Step rows have status icons, step names, durations, expand chevrons, monospace log lines with timestamps/line numbers, highlighted search matches, and copy/permalink line actions.
behavior: Clicking a step expands/collapses its log chunk. Search filters/highlights matching lines and previous/next jumps between matches. Options support show timestamps, show raw logs, copy permalink, and download log archive. Expired/deleted logs show a 410-style unavailable state. Streaming in-progress logs auto-refreshes without losing scroll position.
data_model: Reads workflow_jobs, workflow_steps, workflow_logs, workflow_annotations, repository_permissions. Writes no run state; may write per-user log display preferences. Downloads read log archives from S3 through signed URLs.

description: Implement Actions execution backend for workflow discovery, event triggers, runners, logs, artifacts, caches, and check-run integration.
ui_details: No standalone UI beyond status/check surfaces. Provides API-backed data for Actions tabs, PR checks, repository status badges, artifact downloads, cache management, and future settings pages.
behavior: Rust discovers workflow YAML from .github/workflows, validates triggers, queues runs on push/pull_request/issue_comment/workflow_dispatch, creates check suites/runs, starts isolated ECS Fargate runner jobs, streams logs to S3/Postgres index, uploads artifacts/caches to S3, updates statuses in real time, enforces cancel/rerun/retention limits, and returns structured errors for invalid workflows or unauthorized operations.
data_model: Reads/writes actions_workflows, workflow_runs, workflow_run_attempts, workflow_jobs, workflow_steps, workflow_logs, workflow_artifacts, workflow_caches, workflow_annotations, workflow_dispatch_inputs, check_suites, check_runs, repository_git_refs, commits, pull_requests, repository_permissions, audit records.

description: Implement global search bar, scoped query-builder modal, autocomplete suggestions, command-style jumps, and saved-search creation.
ui_details: Signed-in header search button displays tokenized query text and opens a Primer-style modal with Search heading, combobox, syntax tips link, feedback button, categorized suggestions, and saved-search management. Suggestions include scoped repository/org/all-GitHub search rows, direct repository/code/symbol jump rows, qualifier autocomplete rows for prefixes like language:, and saved-search rows. Saved-search modal is titled `Create saved search` and contains required Name and Query inputs, documentation link, Cancel, and Create saved search buttons.
behavior: Clicking the header search or pressing the global search shortcut opens the modal and focuses the combobox. Typing updates suggestions in place; Enter submits to /search, direct jump rows navigate to their target, qualifier rows autocomplete the token, Escape closes the modal, and saved-search creation validates required fields before persisting. Copilot chat suggestions should be omitted in opengithub MVP.
data_model: Reads repositories, organizations, users, teams, saved_searches, repository_file_index, code_symbol_index, and viewer permissions for suggestions. Writes saved_searches, recent_searches, and optional search telemetry. Never exposes private suggestions unless the viewer has access.

description: Render code search results with type counts, language/path facets, query chips, file snippets, line anchors, and result expansion.
ui_details: Two-pane search page with scrollable left filter rail, draggable splitter, and main results pane. Filter rail shows result type counts for Code, Issues, Pull requests, Discussions, Commits, Packages, Wikis; code facets for Languages and Paths; Advanced controls for Owner, Symbol, Exclude archived, and Advanced search. Main header shows file count, query duration, active scope chips, Save button, type menu, column/view option buttons, and feedback. Result cards group matches by file with collapse button, path link, language badge, match count, snippet table with line-number anchors, highlighted terms, and Show N more matches link.
behavior: Submitting a query fetches permission-filtered code results and facets. Clicking language/path facets adds qualifiers and refreshes URL/results. Backspace/delete on a scope chip removes that qualifier. Collapse hides a file's snippets. Show more expands hidden matches. Line anchors open the blob at that line. Invalid qualifiers, too-large queries, or unsupported regex return inline structured errors without losing typed query.
data_model: Reads repository_file_index, code_symbol_index, git blob metadata, repositories, repository_permissions, languages, paths, saved_searches, and search query parser output. Writes no code data; may write recent_searches/search telemetry. Search index is built from default branch git trees and must enforce visibility.

description: Render issues and pull request search results with shared search shell, result-type switching, advanced facets, sort menus, snippets, labels, and pagination.
ui_details: Same two-pane search shell as code search. Issues/PR rail shows State and Advanced filters: Owner, State, Close reason, Has linked pull request, Author, Assignee, Mentioned user, Mentioned team, Commenter, Involved user, Label, Milestone, Number of comments, Number of interactions, and Advanced search. Main pane shows count, timing, active scope chips, Sort by button, Save button, view options, and dense rows with repository, title, highlighted matches/snippet, label pills, author, opened/updated date, comment count, and issue/PR number.
behavior: Type menu switches between Code, Issues, Pull requests, Discussions, Commits, Packages, and Wikis while preserving the query. Filters add/remove qualifiers. Sort menu supports Best match, Most/Least commented, Newest, Oldest, Recently/Least recently updated and updates URL/results. Clicking a result opens the issue or PR detail. Pagination preserves query, type, sort, and qualifiers.
data_model: Reads issue_search_documents, pull_request_search_documents, issues, pull_requests, repositories, labels, users, comments counts, reactions/interactions, linked PR relationships, milestones, assignees, and permissions. Writes no issue/PR data; may write recent_searches and saved_searches through search-002.

description: Implement permission-aware search backend, indexing, query parser, facets, saved searches, and repository file finder support.
page: Rust API /api/search/* and repository Go to file
ui_details: No standalone page beyond API-backed search surfaces. Supports global search result pages, header suggestions, saved-search modal, repository Go to file, and future advanced search. Repository file finder UI is compact and focused from Go to file or `t`, listing files/directories with path subtitles.
behavior: Rust parses bare terms, quoted strings, boolean AND/OR/NOT, parentheses, bounded regex, and qualifiers such as repo, org, user, language, license, path, symbol, content, is, state, label, author, assignee, milestone, comments, interactions, and sort. Search enforces viewer permissions before returning counts/facets/snippets, indexes default branches first, excludes generated/vendor-like file finder paths unless .gitattributes overrides, and returns structured 422/413 errors for unsupported or oversized queries.
data_model: Reads/writes saved_searches, recent_searches, search_queries, repository_file_index, code_symbol_index, repository_search_documents, issue_search_documents, pull_request_search_documents, commit_search_documents, repositories, repository_permissions, users, labels, milestones, and git refs/blobs. Uses Postgres tsvector and pg_trgm indexes for MVP.

description: Render public user profile overview with identity card, follow/block/report controls, tabs, pinned items, achievements, and contribution graph.
ui_details: Primer-style profile shell with global header, left column avatar/name/username/bio/company/location/follower counts, Follow button, achievements badges, block/report menu, and main column tabs: Overview, Repositories count, Projects, Packages, Stars count, More. Overview shows up to six pinned repository/gist cards in a two-column grid and an accessible contribution heatmap with month labels, intensity legend, annual total, and year selector.
behavior: Click Follow toggles follow state for signed-in users and updates follower count optimistically with rollback on error. Block/report opens a menu and login-gated modal if signed out. Tabs navigate by URL without losing identity column. Contribution cells expose date/count labels to screen readers and hover tooltips. Private profiles hide achievements, activity overview/feed, contribution graph, follower counts, follow/sponsor buttons, org memberships, and secondary tabs while keeping README, bio, and avatar visible.
data_model: Reads users, user_profile_readmes, profile_pins, repositories, repository_languages, stars/forks counts, follows, profile_contribution_days, profile_contribution_events, achievements, user_achievements, organization_memberships, and viewer permissions. Writes follows and user_blocks/report records; profile pin writes are owned by profile settings.

description: Render user repository and starred repository tabs with search, type, language, sort filters, badges, metadata, and URL-backed state.
ui_details: Same profile identity shell as profiles-001. Main tab content has a compact filter row with search input, Type menu, Language menu, Sort menu, and dense repository result rows. Repository rows show name, visibility, archive/fork/template badges, fork source where relevant, description, language color/name, star count, fork count, license, and updated date. Stars tab uses the same row component but represents repositories the user starred and offers sort options Recently starred, Recently active, and Most stars.
behavior: Typing in the search box filters repositories and updates query state. Type menu supports All, Sources, Forks, Archived, Can be sponsored, Mirrors, Templates. Language menu filters by available languages. Sort menu supports Last updated, Name, Stars for repositories and Recently starred, Recently active, Most stars for stars. Clicking a repository opens its Code tab. Empty filters show a no-results state with Clear filters.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_languages, repository_topics, stars, users, forks, licenses, issue/PR counts, and denormalized repository search text. Writes no repository data; may write recent_visits when a row is opened.

description: Render organization overview profile with verified identity, pinned repositories, repository preview, people preview, topics, languages, and org tabs.
ui_details: Organization profile header with logo/avatar, display name, description, Verified badge with verified domain tooltip, Sponsor button when enabled, follower count, website/social links, and tab bar: Overview, Repositories count, Projects, Packages, People count, Sponsoring count, More. Overview shows pinned repository grid, repository preview list, people preview, sponsoring preview, top languages, most-used topic pills, and GitHub Sponsor CTA.
behavior: Verified badge links to verification explanation. Sponsor button is hidden or disabled in opengithub MVP unless sponsorships are implemented. Tabs navigate to org-specific routes. Pinned repository cards and repository preview rows open repositories. Public view shows public repositories and public members only; private/internal details require membership/admin permission.
data_model: Reads organizations, organization_verified_domains, organization_memberships, profile_pins, repositories, repository_languages, repository_topics, stars/forks/issues/PR counts, packages, projects placeholders, follows, sponsorship placeholders, and viewer permissions. Writes follows if org follow is supported later; no admin writes in overview.

description: Render organization repository list and public people list with org-scoped filters, display density controls, pagination, and permission-aware membership visibility.
ui_details: Org repository tab uses org header/tabs plus filter chips for All, Contributed by me, Admin access, Public, Sources, Forks, Archived, Templates; search input; Type/Language/Sort controls; comfortable/compact display-density icon buttons; and rows with repo name, visibility, description, topic pills, language/license, fork/star/issue/PR counts, and updated date. People page uses org tabs, a left Organization permissions side nav, Members subtab, member rows with avatar/name/username, and Previous/Next pagination.
behavior: Repository filters are URL-backed and combine with search, language, sort, and density state. Clicking density changes row spacing without changing data. People pagination preserves org/member filters. Signed-out users see public members only. Org admins later see private members, pending invitations, outside collaborators, role filters, and management actions in settings/admin pages.
data_model: Reads organizations, organization_memberships, users, teams, repositories, repository_permissions, repository_languages, repository_topics, stars/forks/issues/PR counts, licenses, and viewer role. Writes no org data in public list views; may write recent_visits when opening a repository or member.
